Name: curvature_aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Neural Network Inference
Short Hypothesis: By dynamically allocating computational precision based on curvature profiles of neural network layers, we can achieve significant computational savings without compromising model performance.
Related Work: Existing work on mixed-precision neural networks focuses primarily on static precision allocation or heuristic-based approaches. Recent advancements in training-free methods, such as those in 'Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation', have shown promise but lack a formal curvature-aware framework. Our proposal distinguishes itself by integrating curvature diagnostics directly into precision routing decisions, providing a mathematically grounded approach.
Abstract: We propose a novel framework for curvature-aware precision routing in neural networks, aimed at optimizing computational efficiency during inference. By leveraging curvature profiles of network layers, we dynamically allocate computational precision, reducing resource usage without sacrificing performance. Our approach employs training-free diagnostics to quantify gradient covariance decay, Hessian conditioning, and feature entanglement, enabling precise control over precision allocation. We validate our framework through extensive experiments on benchmark datasets, demonstrating significant improvements in computational efficiency while maintaining model accuracy.
Experiments: Evaluate the framework on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets using ResNet architectures.
Compare computational efficiency and model accuracy against baseline mixed-precision methods.
Measure the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on inference time and memory usage.
Perform ablation studies to assess the contribution of individual curvature diagnostics.
Validate the robustness of the framework across different neural network architectures.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential instability in curvature diagnostics for extremely deep networks.
Limited applicability to architectures with non-standard layer structures.
Possible overhead from curvature computation during inference.
Need for careful calibration of precision thresholds to avoid performance degradation.
